name: UNDIFFERENTIATED SARCOMA OF LIVER
definition: UNDIFFERENTIATED EMBRYONAL SARCOMA (UES) = MALIGNANT MESENCHYMOMA / SARCOMA rare highly malignant tumor of children

demographics.Prevalence: 4th-5th most common liver tumor in children
demographics.Age: < 2 months (in 5%); 6-10 years (in 52%); by 15 years (in 90%); up to 49 years; M÷F =
demographics.Path: well-demarcated predominantly solid tumor mixed with cystic gelatinous areas and foci
demographics.Histo: primitive undifferentiated stellate / spindle-shaped sarcomatous cells closely packed in
demographics.Location: right lobe (75%); left lobe (10%); both lobes (15%)

dominant_finding: cystic multiseptate mass of predominantly water attenuation (88% of tumor volume) due to
distribution: right lobe (75%); left lobe (10%); both lobes (15%)
discriminating_features: cystic multiseptate mass of predominantly water attenuation (88% of tumor volume) due to
peripheral foci of soft tissue / septa of variable thickness
± central foci of high attenuation (= hemorrhage)
well-defined margins of mass ← dense enhancing peripheral rim of pseudocapsule

US: large intrahepatic typically iso- to hyperechoic solid mass
cystic areas up to 4 cm in diameter (foci of necrosis + old hemorrhage + cystic myxoid
CT: cystic multiseptate mass of predominantly water attenuation (88% of tumor volume) due to
peripheral foci of soft tissue / septa of variable thickness
± central foci of high attenuation (= hemorrhage)
well-defined margins of mass ← dense enhancing peripheral rim of pseudocapsule
CECT: predominantly peripheral enhancement on delayed images
MR: focal mass with multiple cystic spaces, septations (best seen on MRI) and central necrosis
predominantly CSF signal intensity on T1WI + T2WI
hypointense rim on T1WI + T2WI (= fibrous pseudocapsule)
hyperintense areas of hemorrhage on T1WI
ANGIO: hypo- / hypervascular with stretching of vessels

clinical.Rx: multiagent chemotherapy followed by resection / liver transplantation
